pkg/framework/tracex.purpose: Provides a generic, type-safe OTel span wrapper that automatically records errors, sets span status, and handles panics. Eliminates boilerplate span lifecycle management from all domain services.

patterns.name: Span[T] for value-returning operations
patterns.description: Use tracex.Start[T] + Span.Wrap when the wrapped function returns (T, error). Status is set to Ok or Error automatically; panic is recorded and re-panicked after span.End().
patterns.example: span := tracex.Start[*billing.Invoice](ctx, tracer, "billing.GetInvoice"); return span.Wrap(func(ctx context.Context) (*billing.Invoice, error) { return adapter.Get(ctx, id) })

patterns.name: SpanNoValue for error-only operations
patterns.description: Use tracex.StartWithNoValue + SpanNoValue.Wrap when the wrapped function returns only error. Internally delegates to Span[any].Wrap to share status-setting logic.
patterns.example: span := tracex.StartWithNoValue(ctx, tracer, "billing.DeleteInvoice"); return span.Wrap(func(ctx context.Context) error { return adapter.Delete(ctx, id) })

patterns.name: Options via functional option pattern
patterns.description: Customise the OkStatusDescription via tracex.WithOkStatusDescription(desc) passed as variadic opts to Wrap. Default is "success".

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — defines Span[T], SpanNoValue, Start, StartWithNoValue, Wrap, and Options. Only file in the package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Span.Wrap always calls span.End() via defer-on-panic and explicit call after fn; do not call span.End() outside Wrap or the span will be double-ended.

anti_patterns: Calling tracer.Start directly and managing span.End/RecordError manually — use tracex.Start + Wrap instead
Introducing context.Background() inside Wrap callbacks — the Span carries the correct child ctx; always pass s.ctx through
Adding new Span variants that don't delegate to Span[any].Wrap — SpanNoValue already shows the correct delegation pattern

decisions.decision: Generic Span[T] instead of a single untyped wrapper
decisions.rationale: Preserves compile-time type safety for the return value while sharing the identical error-recording and panic-recovery logic across all call sites.
The span must be closed (exported) before the stack unwinds further, but the caller's defer/recover chain must still see the original panic.

code_examples.scenario: Wrap a DB read in a named span with automatic error recording

pkg/framework/transaction.purpose: Database-agnostic transaction abstraction: propagates a Driver (commit/rollback/savepoint) through context, and provides Run/RunWithNoValue helpers that start-or-reuse a transaction, set savepoints for nested calls, and rollback on error or panic.

patterns.name: Run[R] for transactional value-returning operations
patterns.description: Call transaction.Run(ctx, creator, cb) to execute cb inside a transaction. If a Driver is already in ctx it is reused (nested savepoint); otherwise creator.Tx starts a new one.
patterns.example: result, err := transaction.Run(ctx, a.db, func(ctx context.Context) (*Entity, error) { return a.insertRow(ctx) })

patterns.name: RunWithNoValue for error-only transactional operations
patterns.description: Use transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, creator, cb) when the callback returns only error. Delegates to Run[interface{}] internally.
patterns.example: err := transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, a.db, func(ctx context.Context) error { return a.deleteRow(ctx, id) })

patterns.name: Driver propagation via context key
patterns.description: SetDriverOnContext stores a Driver under a package-private key; GetDriverFromContext retrieves it. A DriverConflictError is returned if a driver is already set, which Run uses to detect and reuse the existing tx.
patterns.example: ctx, err := transaction.SetDriverOnContext(ctx, tx)  // called internally by Run; adapters should NOT call this directly

patterns.name: Savepoints for nested transactions
patterns.description: manage() calls tx.SavePoint() before cb and tx.Rollback() on error, enabling partial rollback within an outer transaction. Commit() is only called when the outermost Run succeeds.
patterns.example: // Implicit — transaction.Run calls manage which calls tx.SavePoint() automatically

patterns.name: Creator interface for adapter integration
patterns.description: Any adapter that can start a transaction implements transaction.Creator via a Tx(ctx) method returning (ctx, Driver, error). Wire injects the concrete Creator (e.g. entutils adapter) into services.
patterns.example: type Creator interface { Tx(ctx context.Context) (context.Context, Driver, error) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines the typed context key, GetDriverFromContext, SetDriverOnContext, DriverNotFoundError, and DriverConflictError. DriverConflictError signals an existing tx in ctx — Run treats this as tx-reuse, not a hard error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SetDriverOnContext returns DriverConflictError (not nil) if a driver is already present — Run explicitly ignores this error type to reuse the existing tx.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Driver, Creator interfaces and Run/RunWithNoValue/getTx/manage. The manage function owns panic recovery (rollback + re-panic), savepoint creation, and commit/rollback on error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: manage() uses defer+recover to rollback and re-panic on any panic — never suppress this panic in adapter code. getTx reuses the existing Driver from ctx silently if present.

anti_patterns: Calling creator.Tx() directly in adapter code instead of using transaction.Run — bypasses savepoint and rollback logic
Storing a *entdb.Tx or raw DB client on adapter structs and using them directly — falls off the ctx-bound transaction
Calling SetDriverOnContext manually outside of transaction.Run — Run already handles this; double-setting will return DriverConflictError and break nesting
Using context.Background() inside a Run callback — loses the Driver already stored in ctx, breaking nested transaction reuse
Ignoring DriverConflictError from SetDriverOnContext outside of the Run helper — the error signals tx reuse and must not be treated as fatal

decisions.decision: Driver interface with SavePoint/Commit/Rollback instead of exposing *sql.Tx directly
decisions.rationale: Keeps the transaction package DB-agnostic; Ent, pgx, and any future driver can implement Driver without leaking ORM types into the framework layer.

decisions.decision: Savepoints used for every nested Run call
decisions.rationale: Allows partial rollback of nested operations while keeping the outer transaction alive, which is required for multi-step charge advancement and billing invoice mutations.

decisions.decision: Panic recovery inside manage() rolls back then re-panics
decisions.rationale: The DB transaction must be cleaned up even on unexpected panics, but the panic must propagate so upper-layer recovery (Chi middleware, test harness) still sees it.

code_examples.scenario: Adapter method that must run inside a transaction, reusing an existing one from ctx if present
